node.text:
If you asked me the best brands in Japan here’s what i’d tell you...🛍️💸 #japantravel

The matcha tier list is just a general guide - your own taste might be different but hopefully its a helpful starting point 🫶

And yeah i’ve consolidated a few posts into this one so hopefully its easier to save and use later :)

Photo by itsjustinjapan on February 03, 2026. May be an image of money, card and text that says '6) Cheapest Lifestyle & Household Items ￥500+ HANDS If you're going to bring home souvenir can guarantee you'll find something here. They sell everything from stationary japanese carpentry tools to kitchenware. ￥300+ 3COINS would describe this as the more premium version of Daiso. Everything has nice clean aesthetic. ￥100+ DAISO JAPAN They carry very different items in Japan so definitely worth trip! Plus it's the cheapest "dollar" store SARIA pa-CEOBTAR'.

Photo by itsjustinjapan on February 03, 2026. May be pop art of magazine, poster and text that says '7) Best Stationary & Art Supplies Loft & Hands have a good variety of mechanical pencils, planners, pens etc. You can find them in most big malls. L.Ft HANDS HANDS Sekaido is a massive 7-storey building that is the designer/artist' /artist's dream shop. Features markers, color pencils, canvases, , notebooks, brushes, pens, frames & hundreds of other things you didn't know you even needed. Prices are very reasonable. 起界堂 書募の SERALDO 500 SARIA สา T2ANE.103122'.

Photo by itsjustinjapan on February 03, 2026. May be an image of childrens toy, drill press, musical instrument, hand drill, sharpener, screwdriver, wrench and text that says '8) Best Knives Kappabashi Street is where chefs go to get their knives. Lots of shops selling high-quality knives here- Tsubaya is fairly large and has a good shopping . You can take your time to hold the knife to make sure it suits you & get your name engraved. hco IN PRAN HIL TREVEL TREVEL11312A'.

Photo by itsjustinjapan on February 03, 2026. May be an image of sneakers, poster and text that says '9) Japanese Sneaker Brands Onitsuka Tiger Founded in Kobe, Onitsuka specialize in thin-soled athletic style shoes. Prices are around ￥15,00 for their standard designs, ￥30,000f ￥30, 000 for their made-in-Japar line. Very comfy. Spingle Spingle flats are made in Hiroshima, from materials such as kangaroo leather. Soft as a feather but tough as nails. Expect to ay around ￥25,000. Goyemon Goyemon combines traditional 'geta' sandals with sneaker soles making them comfortable to wear for long periods. All made-in Japan, retail for around ￥30,000. APHΑ GIN TRAVEL'.

Photo by itsjustinjapan on February 03, 2026. May be an image of text that says '10) Japanese Selvedge Denim Brands Sugar Cane Denim of Sugar Cane's denim is milled in Japan at their own textile mill and sewn in Japan as well. One of the best bang for your buck. Momotaro Jeans Known for its pink inseam and peach boy patch. Uses 100% Zimbabwe cotton and is very smooth, uniform and rigid. You cannot wrong with the denim or construction. Samurai Jeans More interesting fabric choices, colors slub than other brands. Known for its fast- fading, short-fiber heavyweight denim. They started their own cotton farm to ensure all starts with the highest quality materials.'.
